Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick assessment\n\n- No obvious inconsistencies in the latest dosing parameters.\n- CU-001 and CU-002 appear clinically stable/responding based on notes in the config.\n- CU-003 is newly enrolled and on a more frequent schedule (q2w), so this patient may warrant the closest near-term monitoring.\n- From the dosing config alone, PK values appear populated and plausible for all three patients.\n\n## Caveat\n\nThis assessment is based only on the latest dosing configuration file and does **not** independently verify live PK model runtime status, recent recalculations, or monitoring alerts.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and drafted a summary for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary:

- Compound: NXB-7042
- Indication: Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- Source file: `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`
- Last updated: 2026-03-28

Patient dosing
- CU-001: 12.4 kg, 0.15 mg/kg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14
  - Calculated dose: 1.86 mg
  - PK clearance: 3.2 mL/min
  - Note: Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan

- CU-002: 9.1 kg, 0.12 mg/kg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12
  - Calculated dose: 1.09 mg
  - PK clearance: 2.8 mL/min
  - Note: Responding well; weight gain improving

- CU-003: 15.7 kg, 0.18 mg/kg, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10
  - Calculated dose: 2.83 mg
  - PK clearance: 4.1 mL/min
  - Note: New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27

PK stability
- From the dosing config, nothing looks obviously out of range or internally inconsistent.
- CU-001 and CU-002 look clinically stable/responding based on the notes.
- CU-003 is the main one to watch near-term since they’re newly enrolled and on the more frequent q2w schedule.
- That said, this file alone doesn’t confirm live PK model health or alert status — only that the latest stored dosing parameters look coherent.

I’ve also saved a report titled:
- Project Helios Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary

If you want, I can also format this into a short email blurb for Dr. Martinez.
thinking:
**Calculating medication doses**

I’m calculating the per-dose mg based on weight and dose per kg. For CU-001, that’s 12.4 times 0.15, which is about 1.86 mg. CU-002 is 9.1 times 0.12, coming to around 1.09 mg. For CU-003, it's 15.7 times 0.18, giving about 2.83 mg. I should also mention the dosing frequency and next dose dates. Since the user wants a quick summary for Dr. Martinez, I’ll think about using the write_report function.
